criteria.name: planner_controls_keyboard
criteria.description: Sidebar day rows, stop rows, stop form fields, place-detail tabs, map chrome, Export Download/Copy, Import, role switcher, and undo/redo are operable with Tab, Shift+Tab, Enter, or Space

criteria.name: conflict_export_focus_management
criteria.description: Conflict-resolution modal and export canvas trap focus while open and return focus to the originating control on dismiss

criteria.name: cover_and_map_chrome_alt
criteria.description: Plan hero cover image and icon-only map/toolbar controls expose descriptive alt text or accessible names

criteria.name: validation_toast_live_regions
criteria.description: Stop validation errors, import errors, demo toasts, and export copy confirmations are shown visually and announced via aria-live regions

criteria.name: stop_form_explicit_labels
criteria.description: Stop create/edit fields (title, day, location, startTime, endTime, category, costTier, status) use explicit labels associated with their inputs

criteria.name: planner_heading_order
criteria.description: Planner headings (trip title, day sections, export canvas) follow a logical order with no skips such as H1 to H3

criteria.name: planner_landmarks
criteria.description: The workspace exposes landmark regions such as nav for the sidebar and main for the plan column, or an equivalent skip-to-content path

criteria.name: coastal_theme_contrast
criteria.description: Body text, day labels, and primary controls meet readable contrast in both light and dark themes

criteria.name: sidebar_main_button_semantics
criteria.description: Sidebar navigation, plan main region, and primary actions use semantic nav/main/button roles rather than unlabeled generic containers

criteria.name: planner_reduced_motion
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion set, toast, list, coachmark, and control transitions are removed or substantially reduced while create, edit, delete, export, and import stay usable

criteria.name: keyboard_alternative_for_drag
criteria.description: Every drag interaction has a keyboard path: each stop card offers keyboard-operable Move to day and reorder controls that achieve the same reassignment and reordering as a pointer drag

criteria.name: conflict_dialog_semantics
criteria.description: The conflict-resolution modal has role dialog with aria-modal, traps focus while open, makes Keep mine / Take theirs / Merge operable from the keyboard, and returns focus to the edited block on close

criteria.name: poll_names_and_promotion_announced
criteria.description: Poll controls expose accessible names that include the item name and current vote count, and a promotion is announced through an aria-live region

criteria.name: role_state_programmatic
criteria.description: The role switcher exposes the current role programmatically, and controls disabled by the Viewer role are disabled in the accessibility tree rather than merely styled as inactive

criteria.name: export_import_keyboard_operable
criteria.description: Export Download/Copy controls and the trip JSON import control are reachable and operable with the keyboard alone, with a visible focus indicator

criteria.name: multi_facet_round_trip
criteria.description: Multi-facet round-trip: create a stop, switch to Map mode, set the role to Viewer then back to Owner, toggle dark theme, and apply a day filter, then reload. All facets coherently reset to the seeded baseline (seeded stops, Overview/Plan List, Owner, light theme, no day filter) — never a mix where some facets survive

criteria.name: sort_reversal_proves_live_data
criteria.description: Day-order proof: with multi-day stops visible, note the Overview day-section order Sun 7/5 through Sat 7/11; after reordering stops within a day by start time, that day's section and polyline order reverse relative to the prior order, proving order is derived from live stop times rather than a hardcoded list

criteria.name: derived_view_responds_to_input
criteria.description: Derived-view sensitivity: apply a category filter that excludes at least one seeded stop, confirm the plan list and map pins narrow; clear filters and confirm the full plan returns — the derived surfaces change with the filter rather than redrawing identically

criteria.name: cross_view_echo_without_reload
criteria.description: Cross-view echo: rename a stop in Plan List edit and confirm the new title appears in the open place-detail card, the map popup, the kanban card, and the trip JSON preview without a reload

criteria.name: count_delta_is_exact
criteria.description: Count-delta integrity: note the visible stop count and the number of VEVENT blocks in the ICS preview, create one valid scheduled stop, and confirm both counts increase by exactly one with no lag

criteria.name: different_inputs_change_outcomes
criteria.description: Input-dependent output: create two stops with different titles and startTimes on the same day; the day section shows both titles in start-time order, and the ICS payload contains two VEVENT blocks whose SUMMARY and DTSTART differ accordingly

criteria.name: interleaved_flows_preserve_state
criteria.description: Interleaved-flow integrity: start creating stop A, switch to Map mode and open the export canvas, finish creating stop B, then return to the form — stop B is present in list/map/exports and the incomplete A form was not silently submitted

criteria.name: empty_to_repopulated_round_trip
criteria.description: Edge-state round-trip: delete every stop and confirm the empty list state, zero map pins, zero ICS VEVENTs, and an empty trip JSON stops array; then create one valid stop and confirm list, map, ICS, and trip JSON all track back to that single stop

criteria.name: vote_to_export_chain
criteria.description: Chained probe: note a bucket item's name, start its poll and vote it to the winning threshold, confirm it appears as a scheduled stop on a day timeline and as a pin on the map, then open the markdown export and trip JSON and confirm both contain that item's name under the correct day (markdown heading / JSON day field)

criteria.name: merge_content_everywhere_chain
criteria.description: Chained probe: open an edit on a block, trigger the simulated peer edit on the same block, choose Merge in the conflict modal, and confirm the merged content is identical in the block's list row, its open detail card, and the freshly reopened markdown export

criteria.name: undo_round_trip_multi_surface
criteria.description: Chained probe: drag a stop to a different day, note the changed polyline distance labels, press Undo and confirm the stop returns to its original day with the original distance labels and the map pin back in the original day color, then press Redo and confirm the move reapplies

criteria.name: exports_recompile_from_live_state
criteria.description: Input-dependent export: rename a stop and change its start time, then reopen the export surfaces; the markdown contains the new name, the ICS payload's VEVENT for that stop reflects the new DTSTART, and the trip JSON entry shows the new title and startTime — proving all three are recompiled from live state

criteria.name: trip_json_export_import_round_trip
criteria.description: Chained probe: rename a seeded stop and change its startTime, create one new valid stop, Copy or Download the trip JSON, reload to the seeded baseline, Import that JSON, and confirm the renamed title and startTime, the new stop, list rows, map pins, markdown, ICS, and trip JSON all match the pre-export mutated state

criteria.name: field_contract_gates_create_and_export
criteria.description: Chained probe: attempt to create a stop with endTime before startTime and confirm no row is added and endTime is named in the error; then create a valid stop and confirm the trip JSON stops entry and ICS VEVENT for that stop carry the submitted title, day, and startTime

criteria.name: seeded_multi_day_plan_on_load
criteria.description: On load, the planner shows itinerary day sections for Sun 7/5 through Sat 7/11 with at least 8 seeded French Riviera stops (place names visible) distributed across the days, plus at least 3 unscheduled ideas stops not tied to a day

criteria.name: created_stop_appears_in_list
criteria.description: After creating a stop named 'Harbor Lookout' on a day with valid required fields, the text 'Harbor Lookout' is visible in the plan list

criteria.name: create_count_delta_exact
criteria.description: After adding 3 new stops on top of the seed set, the plan list shows exactly 3 more stops than the initial seeded count

criteria.name: place_detail_card_full_tab_set
criteria.description: After selecting a stop, a place-detail card opens over the map showing that stop's name and a tab row with About / Book / Reviews / Photos / Mentions (or equivalent labels for all five)

criteria.name: edited_stop_name_replaces_old
criteria.description: After editing a stop name to 'Evening Promenade', the text 'Evening Promenade' is visible and the previous name is not

criteria.name: deleted_stop_row_removed
criteria.description: After deleting a stop, that stop name is no longer visible in the plan list

criteria.name: empty_state_after_last_delete
criteria.description: After deleting all stops, an empty state message is visible in the plan list region

criteria.name: empty_title_submit_rejected
criteria.description: After submitting the stop form with an empty required title, the stops count is unchanged and an inline validation message naming the title field is visible

criteria.name: day_filter_recomputes_visible_stops
criteria.description: After filtering or focusing a single day (with multi-day stops present), only that day's stops remain emphasized or visible in the filtered list

criteria.name: mode_switch_without_navigation
criteria.description: After switching emphasis between Plan List and Map modes (or Trip plan / map focus), the workspace shows the other mode without full page navigation

criteria.name: detail_tabs_swap_in_place
criteria.description: After switching place detail tabs (About / Reviews or equivalent), the panel content swaps without leaving the page

criteria.name: row_pin_two_way_sync
criteria.description: Starting with the seeded plan visible: click a stop row and confirm the map flies to that stop's pin and the row highlights; then click a different stop's map pin and confirm that stop becomes selected and the plan list scrolls/highlights its row; after each direction the highlighted row and the enlarged pin refer to the same stop

criteria.name: day_select_focus_and_restore_flow
criteria.description: Selecting a day in the sidebar focuses the map on that day's center/zoom, selects the day's first stop, and shows only that day's stops recomputed from the shared collection; then returning to Overview restores the full multi-day plan without a page reload

criteria.name: detail_card_dismiss_closes
criteria.description: The place-detail card opened over the map can be closed with its dismiss control

criteria.name: stops_crud_from_shared_state
criteria.description: Itinerary stops collection supports create, edit, and delete with list rows and derived counts updating from shared application state

criteria.name: two_modes_available
criteria.description: At least two distinct views or interaction modes are switchable without full page navigation

criteria.name: domain_state_beyond_crud
criteria.description: Domain state beyond CRUD is implemented (day filter, mode, selection, or equivalent) and recomputes visible results from the shared collection

criteria.name: create_flow_multi_surface
criteria.description: Full create flow: note a day section's row count and the map's numbered-pin count, create a valid stop assigned to that day, and confirm the day section shows exactly one more stop row and the map shows exactly one more numbered pin in that day's color; then switch between Plan List and Map modes and confirm the same new stop is present in both without a reload

criteria.name: edit_propagates_list_detail_map
criteria.description: With a stop's place-detail card open, edit that stop's name and confirm the new name appears in its day-list row, in the open place-detail card, and in its map pin popup, all without a page reload

criteria.name: delete_clears_row_pin_selection
criteria.description: After selecting a stop and deleting it, its row disappears from the day section, its pin disappears from the map, and the open selection/detail card is cleared, all in the same interaction

criteria.name: reload_restores_seeded_flow
criteria.description: Full reset flow: create one stop and delete a different seeded stop, then reload the page; the app returns to its seeded state — the original seeded stops, day colors, and the default Overview view reappear, with the created stop gone and the deleted stop restored

criteria.name: double_submit_creates_one_stop
criteria.description: Double-activating the stop form submit in quick succession creates exactly one stop: the day section gains exactly one row and the map gains exactly one pin

criteria.name: emptied_day_shows_empty_state
criteria.description: After deleting every stop belonging to one day, selecting that day shows an empty day state message rather than a blank region

criteria.name: map_clears_pins_when_all_deleted
criteria.description: After deleting all stops, the map shows no numbered day pins

criteria.name: long_name_truncates_with_ellipsis
criteria.description: A stop given a name too long for its row truncates with an ellipsis in the list row while the place-detail card shows the full name

criteria.name: live_inline_validation_disables_submit
criteria.description: The stop create/edit form validates as the user types: while a required field is invalid it shows an inline error message naming that field, and the submit control stays disabled until all required fields are valid

criteria.name: inert_chrome_raises_demo_toasts
criteria.description: Inert demo affordances (Share, Optimize route, or map layers) give visible simulated feedback such as a toast when activated, rather than doing nothing or navigating away

criteria.name: plan_hero_title_and_dates
criteria.description: The plan hero shows a cover image, an editable title reading 'Trip to the French Riviera - Cote d'Azur', and the date range 7/5–7/11

criteria.name: top_plan_chrome_inert_affordances
criteria.description: The top plan chrome shows a Trip / Travel Planner brand mark, Trip plan / Trip journal mode labels, and Share and edit toolbar affordances that behave as inert in-app chrome (a toast or no-op) rather than navigating away

criteria.name: sidebar_nav_anatomy_complete
criteria.description: The left nav sidebar shows an Overview entry, an Itinerary day list for Sun 7/5 through Sat 7/11 with a distinct day-color dot per day, a Budget row, and Support and Hide sidebar controls

criteria.name: hover_highlights_paired_marker
criteria.description: Hovering a stop row in the plan column visibly highlights that stop's map marker (enlargement or halo) and emphasizes the route segment through it, and moving the pointer away clears the highlight — verified by real pointer hover

criteria.name: day_focus_fits_map
criteria.description: Activating a day header's Focus control fits the map viewport to exactly that day's stops, and leaving focus mode restores the full-plan map extent

criteria.name: day_polylines_with_recomputed_distances
criteria.description: The map draws one directional polyline per day connecting that day's stops in start-time order in the day's color, with each leg labeled with a distance in kilometers to one decimal; after reordering or reassigning a stop the affected polylines and distance labels update immediately

criteria.name: marker_clustering_by_zoom
criteria.description: Zooming the map out groups nearby pins into cluster badges showing the count of contained stops, and zooming in or clicking a cluster expands it back into individual pins

criteria.name: map_layer_toggle_three_styles
criteria.description: A map layer toggle offers at least three layer styles and switching between them restyles the map in place without a page reload or blank map

criteria.name: lodging_isochrone_toggle
criteria.description: Selecting the seeded Hotel Le Negresco stop offers an isochrone toggle that draws a translucent circle of roughly 1.25 km radius around the hotel, and toggling it off removes the overlay

criteria.name: drag_reassign_and_reorder
criteria.description: Dragging a stop card to another day reassigns it and dragging within a day reorders it, with the day sections, map pins, polylines, and distance labels all reflecting the new arrangement immediately after the drop

criteria.name: day_accordion_collapse_expand
criteria.description: Activating a day section header collapses that day's stop rows and activating it again expands them, while the other days keep their own expanded or collapsed state

criteria.name: time_collision_amber_warning
criteria.description: When two time blocks in the same day overlap in time, both blocks show an amber overlap treatment with a warning icon, and adjusting either block's times so they no longer overlap clears the treatment from both

criteria.name: travel_buffer_mode_recompute
criteria.description: Travel-time buffer cards between consecutive stops show a Driving / Walking / Transit mode selector and a computed duration, and changing the mode immediately changes the displayed duration consistent with a faster mode yielding a shorter buffer

criteria.name: impossible_transit_warning
criteria.description: When the gap between two consecutive blocks is shorter than the selected mode's computed buffer, the buffer card shows an impossible-transit warning, and widening the gap or choosing a faster mode clears it

criteria.name: timezone_axis_relabels_times
criteria.description: The timezone switcher toggles displayed block times between destination local (CET), home (ET), and UTC: every visible time label relabels to the selected zone and the switcher indicates the active zone

criteria.name: recurring_generator_creates_seven_blocks
criteria.description: Creating a stop such as 'Breakfast at hotel' 8:00-8:45 with the repeat-daily option enabled creates exactly one block per day across Sun 7/5 through Sat 7/11 (7 blocks) in a single action

criteria.name: bucket_drawer_with_polls
criteria.description: The unassigned-concepts bucket drawer opens from a persistent control and lists the unscheduled ideas, each carrying a poll widget with per-member vote indicators for Sarah, John, and Marco plus a user vote control

criteria.name: vote_winner_promotes_to_timeline
criteria.description: After starting a vote on a bucket item, the simulated members cast votes with visible count updates, and when the item reaches 3 of 4 votes it leaves the bucket and appears as a scheduled stop on a day timeline with a map pin in that day's color

criteria.name: conflict_modal_choice_applies
criteria.description: Triggering a simulated peer edit on a block the user has an unsaved edit open on opens a conflict-resolution modal showing current and incoming versions side by side with Keep mine / Take theirs / Merge choices, and the chosen resolution is actually applied to the block

criteria.name: role_switcher_gates_controls
criteria.description: Switching the role to Viewer visibly disables all mutating controls (create, edit, delete, drag, vote, bulk), Editor keeps delete disabled while create/edit/move work, and Owner enables everything

criteria.name: activity_log_records_mutations
criteria.description: The activity log drawer lists session mutations newest first, each entry naming the actor and the change, and performing a create, a drag move, and a delete appends three matching entries

criteria.name: filter_ribbon_combines_and_clears
criteria.description: The filter ribbon combines cost-tier, category, and tag filters to recompute the visible stops from the shared collection, and Clear filters restores the full plan

criteria.name: fuzzy_search_typo_tolerant
criteria.description: Typing a slightly misspelled query such as 'anitbes' in the search field still surfaces the Antibes stop, and clearing the query restores the unfiltered view

criteria.name: bulk_selection_bar_actions
criteria.description: Selecting multiple stops summons a bulk action bar showing the selected count, and its Mass tag, Move to day, and Delete actions apply to every selected stop in one operation

criteria.name: kanban_pivot_status_columns
criteria.description: Kanban pivot mode shows the stops as To Visit / Reserved / Completed columns, and dragging a card between columns updates that stop's status wherever it appears

criteria.name: markdown_export_live_compile
criteria.description: The export canvas shows a markdown itinerary with a heading per day in date order and time-ordered stop lines, and reopening it after a change shows the updated document with a Copy control that confirms via toast

criteria.name: ics_payload_valid_structure
criteria.description: The ICS view renders a payload that begins with BEGIN:VCALENDAR, contains exactly one VEVENT block per scheduled stop with a DTSTART and a SUMMARY naming the stop, and ends with END:VCALENDAR

criteria.name: undo_redo_structural_changes
criteria.description: Undo and redo controls step through structural changes: after a delete, Undo restores the stop across list, map, and kanban together and Redo removes it again

criteria.name: coachmarks_sequence_first_load
criteria.description: On a fresh load an onboarding coachmark sequence of at least 3 steps points at the plan column, the map pane, and the ideas bucket with Next and Skip controls, and once dismissed it does not reappear during the session

criteria.name: theme_toggle_swaps_all_panes
criteria.description: The light/dark toggle swaps the palette across the sidebar, plan column, and map chrome without a reload, with text readable in both themes

criteria.name: stop_field_contract_enforced
criteria.description: The stop create/edit form enforces the Stop field contract: empty title, endTime not after startTime, and out-of-enum category/costTier/status/day each show an inline error naming that field and leave the stops count unchanged; a valid submit creates a record whose visible title, day, location, times, category, costTier, and status match the submitted values

criteria.name: trip_json_live_compile
criteria.description: The export canvas trip JSON preview is a live-compiled document with schemaVersion "1", a trip object carrying title and dateStart/dateEnd, and a stops array; after creating or renaming a stop the JSON updates to include that stop's title and fields without a reload

criteria.name: trip_json_download_and_copy
criteria.description: Download trip JSON offers a real download of the visible trip JSON text, and Copy trip JSON places that exact text on the clipboard with a visible confirmation toast

criteria.name: import_trip_json_reconstructs
criteria.description: Importing a previously exported trip JSON reconstructs the stops so list rows, map pins, and the trip JSON preview match the imported document; importing malformed JSON shows a visible error and leaves the session unchanged

criteria.name: ics_download_control
criteria.description: Download ICS offers a real download of the visible ICS payload that begins with BEGIN:VCALENDAR and contains one VEVENT per scheduled stop

criteria.name: spacing_and_sizing_follow_scale
criteria.description: Spacing and sizing follow the design system's scale, with no arbitrary one-off values visible in computed styles.

criteria.name: typography_matches_spec
criteria.description: Typography matches the spec for all headings and body copy.

criteria.name: layout_matches_reference
criteria.description: Layout matches reference screenshots to within a small tolerance at all specified breakpoints; fidelity tasks achieve near pixel-parity.

criteria.name: specified_state_changes_animate
criteria.description: Transitions or animations are applied to the state changes the spec calls out, such as add, delete, filter, or view switch.

criteria.name: responsive_behavior_matches_reference
criteria.description: Responsive behavior matches reference patterns.

criteria.name: control_styling_matches_spec
criteria.description: Button and form radii, padding, and shadows conform to the spec.

criteria.name: typography_has_clear_hierarchy
criteria.description: Typography has a clear hierarchy that distinguishes section and body text.

criteria.name: component_states_match_spec
criteria.description: Component states (default, hover, active, disabled) match the spec where specified.

criteria.name: surface_treatments_match_spec
criteria.description: Color, depth, and border treatments are precise per the design.

criteria.name: microinteractions_match_spec
criteria.description: Microinteraction feedback for hover and press follows the spec in duration and motion.

criteria.name: extended_surfaces_adopt_reference_language
criteria.description: The added surfaces (buffer cards, bucket drawer, kanban columns, conflict modal, export canvas) adopt the reference workspace's spacing scale, radii, and coastal palette so they read as native parts of the screenshotted app

criteria.name: dual_pane_proportions_match_reference
criteria.description: The plan-column card anatomy and map-pane proportions match the reference screenshots, and the added route, cluster, and isochrone layers do not obscure or distort the referenced three-pane layout

criteria.name: empty_plan_and_day_states
criteria.description: After deleting all stops the plan list shows an empty state message, and selecting a day with no stops shows an empty day state rather than a blank region

criteria.name: stop_form_inline_validation
criteria.description: The stop create/edit form shows inline per-field errors for title, endTime, category, costTier, status, and day before submit under the Stop field contract

criteria.name: stop_and_import_errors_actionable
criteria.description: Stop validation and trip JSON import errors name the problem field and a fix (for example endTime must be after startTime) rather than only saying Invalid

criteria.name: export_copy_and_import_feedback
criteria.description: Export Copy and successful Import show a visible confirmation toast or notice, and a failed import shows a visible error

criteria.name: vote_and_peer_sim_feedback
criteria.description: Simulated peer voting and peer-edit conflict resolution show visible progress or state updates (vote counts changing, conflict modal opening) rather than a silent wait with no UI change

criteria.name: delete_supports_undo
criteria.description: After deleting a stop, Undo restores it across list, map, and exports; bulk Delete requires an explicit confirm step before removing selected stops

criteria.name: coachmarks_guide_workspace
criteria.description: The onboarding coachmark sequence and/or tooltips explain the plan column, map pane, and ideas bucket so non-obvious collaboration controls are discoverable

criteria.name: planner_controls_semantic
criteria.description: Stop form fields, export Download/Copy, Import, role switcher, and sidebar day rows use semantic button/input/label elements rather than clickable bare divs

criteria.name: conflict_and_export_dismiss_paths
criteria.description: The conflict-resolution modal and export canvas each offer an operable dismiss or choice path that returns to the plan without trapping the user

criteria.name: coachmark_and_vote_progress
criteria.description: The coachmark sequence shows step progress (Next/Skip), and bucket polls show visible vote-count progress toward the 3-of-4 promotion threshold

criteria.name: collision_flags_and_clears
criteria.description: Editing a block's times to overlap another block flags both amber immediately, and editing them apart clears both warnings

criteria.name: empty_bucket_state
criteria.description: Emptying the ideas bucket shows a bucket empty state message with no orphaned poll widgets remaining

criteria.name: viewer_blocked_action_feedback
criteria.description: In the Viewer role, activating a mutating control changes nothing and a read-only notice explains that Viewers cannot edit the plan

criteria.name: empty_undo_history_safe
criteria.description: With an empty undo history the Undo control is disabled or inert, and activating it produces no state change and no console error

criteria.name: recurring_duplicate_guard
criteria.description: Running the repeat-daily generator a second time for the same title and time warns about the duplicates and adds no additional blocks

criteria.name: end_before_start_rejected
criteria.description: Submitting a stop with endTime not strictly after startTime adds no stop and shows an inline error naming endTime

criteria.name: out_of_enum_rejected
criteria.description: Submitting a stop with a category, costTier, status, or day outside the closed enums adds no stop and shows an inline error naming that field

criteria.name: malformed_import_rejected
criteria.description: Importing malformed trip JSON shows a visible error naming the import problem, leaves stops unchanged, and produces no console errors

criteria.name: invalid_stop_import_rejected
criteria.description: Importing trip JSON whose stops violate the Stop field contract (missing title, illegal enum, or endTime not after startTime) shows a visible error naming the offending field and leaves the plan unchanged

criteria.name: title_or_notes_length_rejected
criteria.description: Submitting a title longer than 120 characters or notes longer than 500 characters adds no stop and shows an inline error naming that field

criteria.name: delightful_microinteractions
criteria.description: Optional: the itinerary planner adds a delightful microinteraction beyond the required list/map animations — for example presence caret drift, polyline draw-on, or export compile pulse

criteria.name: advanced_motion_mechanics
criteria.description: Optional: advanced motion beyond the required set — for example coordinated map fly + pin enlarge choreography or day-section accordion height easing

criteria.name: guided_onboarding
criteria.description: Optional: onboarding goes beyond the required 3-step coachmark — for example a contextual tip after the first vote promotion or first export

criteria.name: enhanced_interactive_graphics
criteria.description: Optional: an extra visualization beyond required polylines/isochrones — for example a day-density strip or distance summary chip

criteria.name: alternative_input_support
criteria.description: Optional: an alternative input path such as keyboard multi-select chord or paste-to-create from confirmation text

criteria.name: preference_personalization
criteria.description: Optional: personalization beyond the required light/dark toggle — for example remembered default transport mode or preferred timezone within the session

criteria.name: polished_brand_narrative
criteria.description: Optional: polished Côte d'Azur storytelling chrome beyond the required brand mark and plan hero

criteria.name: dynamic_theming_beyond_requirements
criteria.description: Optional: theming beyond the required light/dark structural toggle — for example a coastal accent pack switcher

criteria.name: genre_appropriate_platform_features
criteria.description: Optional: a useful web-platform extra such as a print stylesheet polish pass or offline map-cache indicator chrome (still frontend-only)

criteria.name: competition_level_innovation
criteria.description: Optional: a competition-level itinerary innovation visible in-browser beyond the specified collaboration and export depth

criteria.name: latency_simulation_optimistic_ui
criteria.description: The app ships the optional latency simulation control center: choosing 50, 500, or 2000 ms visibly delays simulated peer activity while the UI stays optimistic and settles cleanly

criteria.name: error_toast_test_dispatcher
criteria.description: The app ships the optional error-toast test dispatcher: triggering a simulated parse or sync failure raises a distinct toast carrying a visible recovery or dismiss path

criteria.name: innovation_catchall
criteria.description: The app demonstrates a noteworthy, browser-observable enhancement beyond the spec that is NOT covered by any other criterion in this file. The enhancement must plausibly matter to a real user, not be a nitpick. If present, name the enhancement and cite the concrete evidence (element, page state, screenshot) that demonstrates it. If the enhancement is already covered — even partially — by another criterion in this file, answer no here and let that criterion carry it.

criteria.name: hover_ease_and_press_scale
criteria.description: Hovering sidebar items eases a hover opacity change with a brief press scale on activation, place and carousel cards lift slightly on hover, and map chrome buttons show the same hover/press microfeedback — verified by real pointer hover, not a state shortcut

criteria.name: mode_switch_keeps_hover_feedback
criteria.description: Plan/Map mode switches update without full reload and keep hover feedback

criteria.name: map_fly_and_pin_enlarge
criteria.description: Selecting a stop or a day eases/flies the map to the target and animates the chosen pin into its enlarged active state

criteria.name: list_add_remove_animates
criteria.description: Creating a stop through the form animates the new row into its day section, and deleting a stop animates its row out, rather than rows appearing or vanishing in a single instant frame — verified through the real UI controls, not a state shortcut

criteria.name: day_reassign_reflow_animates
criteria.description: Reassigning a stop to another day through the edit form animates the reflow of both affected day sections as the row leaves one and enters the other

criteria.name: toast_slide_fade_autodismiss
criteria.description: Demo toasts slide or fade in when triggered and auto-dismiss on their own after a short interval

criteria.name: reduced_motion_respected
criteria.description: With prefers-reduced-motion enabled, toast and control transitions are disabled or substantially reduced while the app remains fully usable

criteria.name: detail_tabs_swap_without_navigation
criteria.description: Place-detail tab switches swap panels in place with the detail card overlaying the map, without any page navigation

criteria.name: drag_lift_and_settle
criteria.description: A dragged stop card visibly lifts (elevation or scale) while dragging and settles into its drop slot with a brief ease rather than teleporting, and the vacated and receiving day sections animate their reflow — verified through a real pointer drag

criteria.name: peer_carets_drift_smoothly
criteria.description: The colored peer carets or card outlines drift with smooth continuous easing between positions rather than teleporting in discrete jumps

criteria.name: coachmark_step_transitions
criteria.description: Advancing the onboarding coachmarks with the real Next control transitions between steps with a short fade or slide rather than an instant swap — verified on a fresh load

criteria.name: accordion_height_animates
criteria.description: Collapsing and expanding a day accordion through its real header control animates the height change rather than snapping

criteria.name: promotion_animates_bucket_to_day
criteria.description: A winning poll animates its item out of the bucket and into the day section rather than swapping both surfaces in a single instant frame — verified by running the real vote flow

criteria.name: export_import_notice_motion
criteria.description: Export copy confirmation and import success or error notices appear with a short enter/exit transition rather than popping with no motion (unless prefers-reduced-motion is set)

criteria.name: cold_start_is_under_two_seconds
criteria.description: Cold start to interactive is under 2 seconds on local render.

criteria.name: console_is_clean
criteria.description: Browser devtools show no errors or warnings.

criteria.name: transitions_respond_under_100ms
criteria.description: UI transitions respond in under 100ms.

criteria.name: async_work_has_loading_indicators
criteria.description: Loading indicators are shown for async or simulated delays.

criteria.name: large_collections_render_without_lag
criteria.description: Large collections render without perceived lag.

criteria.name: state_changes_remain_interactive
criteria.description: The UI remains interactive during state changes.

criteria.name: animations_maintain_smooth_frame_rate
criteria.description: Animations maintain a smooth 60fps; games maintain a stable frame rate during play.

criteria.name: rapid_input_does_not_freeze
criteria.description: The app does not hang or freeze even under rapid user input.

criteria.name: drag_smooth_full_grid
criteria.description: Dragging a stop card across the fully seeded multi-day grid tracks the pointer smoothly with no visible stutter or dropped frames

criteria.name: ambient_simulation_stable
criteria.description: After 60 seconds of the presence and caret simulation running, the console shows no new errors and interactions remain as responsive as at load

criteria.name: cluster_zoom_smooth
criteria.description: Rapidly zooming the map through cluster and uncluster levels repeatedly shows no hitching, missing pins, or console errors

criteria.name: three_pane_to_stacked
criteria.description: At 1440px the sidebar, plan column, and map pane sit side by side; below 1024px the map stacks under the plan; at 375px the workspace remains usable without horizontal page scroll

criteria.name: mobile_tap_targets
criteria.description: At 375px width, sidebar toggle, stop rows, and primary Export/Add controls present tap targets at least 44px in at least one dimension

criteria.name: planner_type_scales
criteria.description: Trip title and day headings remain readable at 1440px and at 375px without overflowing their containers

criteria.name: no_viewport_clip
criteria.description: At 1440px and 375px, plan list rows, map pane, and export canvas controls stay within the viewport without clipping primary content

criteria.name: sidebar_drawer_below_768
criteria.description: At widths of 768px and below the left sidebar collapses behind a toggle that opens it as an overlay drawer

criteria.name: map_stacks_below_plan
criteria.description: Below 1024px the map pane stacks below the plan column rather than overlapping or sitting beside a crushed sidebar

criteria.name: mobile_tap_operates_stops
criteria.description: At 375px width, tapping a stop row selects it and tapping the sidebar toggle opens the drawer

criteria.name: no_page_horizontal_scroll_375
criteria.description: At 375px width the page body has no horizontal scrolling; kanban may scroll inside its own container only

criteria.name: map_and_hero_responsive
criteria.description: Plan hero cover image and the map pane resize with the layout at 1440px and 375px without overflowing

criteria.name: toggle_and_export_reachable
criteria.description: Sidebar toggle, Export control, and undo/redo remain reachable at 375px without being covered by fixed chrome

criteria.name: kanban_and_drawers_adapt
criteria.description: Below 1024 px the kanban columns scroll horizontally inside their own container without page-level horizontal scroll, and at 768 px and below the ideas bucket and activity log open as overlay drawers

criteria.name: shared_state_coherence
criteria.description: Stops, day selection, active mode, and place-detail selection behave as one coherent shared state: a change made in any one view (list edit, map selection, day filter) is immediately reflected in every other view showing that data, without a reload

criteria.name: reload_resets_all_facets_coherently
criteria.description: After configuring several independent facets (create a stop, select a day, switch mode, open a detail card), reloading the page returns every facet to the seeded in-memory baseline together — seeded stops, default Overview, no lingering selection — never a mix of surviving and reset facets

criteria.name: console_clean_full_exercise
criteria.description: No console errors or warnings appear during a full exercise of creating, editing, deleting, mode switching, day selection, and map selection

criteria.name: interactive_within_two_seconds
criteria.description: The app is interactive within 2 seconds of a local cold load

criteria.name: rapid_input_stays_smooth
criteria.description: Mode switches, day selection, and map fly animations stay smooth under rapid repeated input, with no hangs, freezes, or dropped interactions

criteria.name: keyboard_operable_with_focus_ring
criteria.description: Every interactive control — sidebar rows, stop rows, form fields, place-detail tabs, and map chrome buttons — is reachable and operable with the keyboard alone, with a visible focus indicator on each

criteria.name: detail_tabs_aria_and_focus_return
criteria.description: The place-detail tab row is keyboard operable with the active tab marked programmatically (e.g. aria-selected), and activating the dismiss control from the keyboard closes the card and returns focus to the stop that opened it

criteria.name: validation_announced_aria_live
criteria.description: Stop form validation messages are shown inline and announced through an aria-live polite region

criteria.name: map_pins_accessible_names
criteria.description: Map pins expose accessible names that include the place name and its day assignment

criteria.name: document_title_names_trip
criteria.description: The browser document title references the French Riviera trip rather than a build-tool or framework default placeholder title

criteria.name: collab_simulation_is_real_state
criteria.description: Simulated collaboration mutates the same shared collection the UI reads: a promoted bucket item becomes a normal stop that can be edited, dragged, and deleted, and simulated mutations appear in the same activity log as the user's own

criteria.name: role_timezone_theme_no_reload
criteria.description: Switching role, timezone mode, or theme updates every affected surface as shared client state without reloading the document

criteria.name: ics_structure_parses
criteria.description: The rendered ICS payload is structurally valid: BEGIN:VCALENDAR and END:VCALENDAR wrap one VEVENT per scheduled stop, and each VEVENT carries a parseable DTSTART and a SUMMARY

criteria.name: clipboard_copy_matches_display
criteria.description: Activating the export Copy control places text on the clipboard that matches the displayed markdown document and raises a visible confirmation

criteria.name: print_layout_clean
criteria.description: Under print media the plan renders as a clean paginated document of day sections with the map chrome, sidebar, and toolbars excluded

criteria.name: trip_json_schema_contract
criteria.description: The trip JSON preview conforms to the field contract: schemaVersion is the string 1, trip carries title/dateStart/dateEnd, and every stops element exposes title, day, location, category, costTier, status, lat, and lng using the closed enums and bounds from the instruction

criteria.name: in_memory_only_no_storage
criteria.description: During a full exercise including export and import, the app does not write to localStorage or sessionStorage; persistence is the portable artifacts and MCP surface only

criteria.name: complete_stop_payload_contract
criteria.description: A created stop and its matching trip JSON element use the same complete payload: title trimmed length 1–120; day one of the seven 2025-07-05 through 2025-07-11 dates or unscheduled; location trimmed length 1–200; optional startTime/endTime as valid HH:MM with endTime only when startTime exists and strictly later; category lodging/food/transit/activity/idea; costTier 1/2/3/4; status to-visit/reserved/completed; optional tags as at most 8 unique trimmed strings of length 1–24; optional notes length at most 500; numeric lat -90 through 90 and lng -180 through 180

criteria.name: ics_event_date_time_semantics
criteria.description: For the live scheduled stops, each ICS VEVENT has SUMMARY exactly equal to its title; a timed stop has DTSTART matching its day and startTime, an untimed stop has DTSTART;VALUE=DATE on its day, and a stop with endTime has DTEND matching that same-day endTime; unscheduled ideas produce no VEVENT

criteria.name: create_flow_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Create flow: creating a valid stop adds exactly one day-list row and one map pin in that day's color and includes the stop in markdown, ICS, and trip JSON without a reload

criteria.name: invalid_create_shows_inline_validation
criteria.description: Create flow: submitting with an empty title or endTime before startTime triggers immediate inline validation naming the offending field and adds no stop

criteria.name: edit_flow_updates_related_displays
criteria.description: Edit flow: renaming a stop updates the day-list row, place-detail card, map popup, markdown export, ICS SUMMARY, and trip JSON title without a reload

criteria.name: delete_flow_updates_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Delete flow: deleting a stop removes it from the day section, map pins, selection, markdown, ICS, and trip JSON in the same interaction

criteria.name: view_switch_retains_state
criteria.description: View/mode switch: switching Plan List and Map modes keeps the same selected stop and shows it in both without a full page reload

criteria.name: last_delete_reveals_empty_state
criteria.description: Edge: after deleting all stops, the plan list empty state is visible, the map shows no numbered pins, and ICS has zero VEVENT blocks

criteria.name: filters_update_all_surfaces
criteria.description: Edge: applying a day or category filter narrows the plan list and map pins together, and Clear filters restores the full plan

criteria.name: collapsible_chrome_preserves_workflow
criteria.description: Collapsible chrome: Hide sidebar collapses the left nav and the toggle reopens it as a drawer or sidebar with the current day selection preserved

criteria.name: overlays_support_expected_flows
criteria.description: Overlays: place-detail, conflict-resolution, and export canvas open and dismiss without trapping the user; dismiss returns to the underlying plan

criteria.name: flow_recovers_without_reload
criteria.description: Recovery: after a failed import or Viewer-blocked action, a visible error or notice appears and the user can dismiss it and continue editing without a reload

criteria.name: drag_reassign_updates_route_and_log
criteria.description: Dragging a stop from one day to another updates the old day section, the new day section, both days' polylines and distance labels, the adjacent buffer cards, and appends an activity log entry describing the move, all without a reload

criteria.name: vote_promotion_end_to_end
criteria.description: Running a bucket poll to its winning threshold moves the item out of the bucket onto the timeline, and the promoted stop then appears in Plan List mode, on the map in its day's color, and under its day heading in the export markdown

criteria.name: conflict_merge_end_to_end
criteria.description: Resolving a simulated edit conflict with the Merge choice leaves the merged content visible in the block's row, in its detail card, and in the export markdown, with a matching activity log entry

criteria.name: undo_redo_round_trip_flow
criteria.description: Deleting a stop then pressing Undo restores it to its day section, its map pin, and the export document, and pressing Redo removes it again from all three

criteria.name: kanban_status_echoes_plan_list
criteria.description: Changing a stop's status by dragging its card between kanban columns is reflected on that stop's row in Plan List mode and in status-filtered results without a reload

criteria.name: role_round_trip_preserves_edits
criteria.description: Switching to Viewer disables all mutating controls, and switching back to Owner re-enables them with every edit made before the switch still intact

criteria.name: export_import_round_trip_flow
criteria.description: Export then import flow: mutate the plan (rename + new stop), Download or Copy trip JSON, reload to seeded baseline, Import that JSON, and confirm list, map, markdown, ICS, and trip JSON match the pre-export mutated state

criteria.name: three_pane_planner_density
criteria.description: Three-pane planner composition (left sidebar / center plan column / right map pane) remains dense and trip-specific, with the planner workspace as the first viewport (no marketing landing)

criteria.name: empty_list_state_visible
criteria.description: Empty plan list state is visually present and clear when no stops remain

criteria.name: day_colored_numbered_pins
criteria.description: The map shows one numbered pin per stop colored by its day, the selected pin is enlarged with a popup reading the place name and its Day N label, and unscheduled ideas stops use a neutral non-day marker

criteria.name: coastal_palette_and_typeface
criteria.description: The page shows a soft coastal look: a cool blue-gray page wash, a humanist sans-serif body typeface, and a navy accent color used consistently across chrome and controls

criteria.name: sidebar_dot_matches_pin_color
criteria.description: Each itinerary day carries its own distinct color, and that color is shared between the day's sidebar dot and its numbered map pins

criteria.name: floating_detail_card_anatomy
criteria.description: The place-detail card floats over the map pane with a visible tab row (About / Book / Reviews / Photos / Mentions) and a dismiss control

criteria.name: single_consistent_icon_set
criteria.description: Icons across the sidebar, toolbar, and map chrome come from one consistent icon set rendered at consistent sizes, with no mixed styles or pixelated glyphs

criteria.name: hero_cover_title_date_range
criteria.description: The plan hero presents a cover image, the trip title 'Trip to the French Riviera - Cote d'Azur', and the date range 7/5–7/11 with clear typographic hierarchy over the plan column

criteria.name: panes_side_by_side_at_1024
criteria.description: At viewport widths of 1024px and above the sidebar, plan column, and map pane render side by side; below 1024px the map pane stacks below the plan column

criteria.name: sidebar_drawer_at_768
criteria.description: At viewport widths of 768px and below, the left sidebar collapses behind a toggle control that opens it as an overlay drawer

criteria.name: ui_copy_quality
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings, labels, empty states, and validation messages, the copy uses one consistent capitalization convention and consistent terminology, and error messages name the problem field rather than a generic rejection

criteria.name: brand_and_trip_signal_first_viewport
criteria.description: The first viewport shows the Trip / Travel Planner product name with French Riviera — Côte d'Azur as the trip signal, and the initial view is the planner workspace rather than a marketing or login screen

criteria.name: dual_pane_route_anatomy
criteria.description: Day-colored route polylines carry small distance labels, cluster badges are circular with a visible count, and the isochrone circle renders translucent so the map beneath stays readable

criteria.name: scheduling_grid_anatomy
criteria.description: Time blocks display their start-end times, overlapping blocks use an amber treatment with a warning icon, and travel buffer cards are visually distinct from stop cards (for example a dashed connector style) so the timeline reads as blocks joined by transit

criteria.name: collaboration_chrome_anatomy
criteria.description: Presence avatars render as overlapping colored circles in the top chrome, peer carets use each member's distinct color, and the conflict modal presents current and incoming versions in two clearly differentiated columns

criteria.name: kanban_filter_bucket_anatomy
criteria.description: Kanban pivot shows three equal-width columns titled To Visit, Reserved, and Completed each with a count badge, the filter ribbon reads as one row of labeled chips above the plan column, and bucket poll cards share the app's card treatment

criteria.name: dark_mode_coherent_panes
criteria.description: In dark mode the sidebar, plan column, and map chrome all swap coherently to the dark palette with readable text throughout

criteria.name: headings_use_consistent_capitalization
criteria.description: Where the app renders headings and section titles, they use consistent capitalization (one convention — title case or sentence case — throughout).

criteria.name: actions_use_specific_labels
criteria.description: Where the app renders button or action labels, they are specific verbs ('Add stop', 'Download ICS', 'Import trip JSON'), not generic ('Submit', 'OK') when a specific label is possible.

criteria.name: errors_name_problem_and_fix
criteria.description: Where the app renders error messages, they name the problem and the fix ('Title is required', 'endTime must be after startTime'), not just a rejection ('Invalid').

criteria.name: empty_states_explain_next_step
criteria.description: Where the app renders empty plan, empty day, or empty ideas-bucket states, the copy explains that no stops remain and how to add one.

criteria.name: body_copy_is_well_written
criteria.description: Where the app renders body or marketing copy, rate how free it is of spelling and grammatical errors.

criteria.name: terminology_is_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders labels for the same concept in multiple places, terminology is consistent (not 'stop' here and 'activity' there for the same row, or 'trip JSON' vs an unrelated export name).

criteria.name: numbers_dates_and_units_are_consistent
criteria.description: Where the app renders numbers, dates, and units, formatting is consistent (day labels 7/5–7/11, distance labels in km to one decimal, times in one clock convention per timezone mode).

criteria.name: success_messages_are_specific
criteria.description: Where the app renders confirmation and success messages, they state what happened ('Trip JSON copied', 'Import complete'), not vague affirmations.

criteria.name: activity_log_entries_specific
criteria.description: Where the activity log renders entries, each is a specific human-readable sentence naming actor, action, and target (for example 'John moved Flight 102 to Day 1'), not a generic 'item updated'

criteria.name: export_markdown_well_formed
criteria.description: Where the markdown export renders, it uses a heading per day and readable time-ordered lines with no raw data dumps, and the coachmark and conflict-modal copy explains what to do in plain language

criteria.name: field_contract_errors_name_fields
criteria.description: Where stop validation or trip JSON import errors render, the copy names the problem field (title, endTime, category, or import) and a fix — not a generic rejection
